metadata.title: Chinese tech cross-ownership in Western media/games/tech + CCP influence vectors

ownership.control: economic in new sub; only ~5% voting
largest external holder, NET SELLER (down from ~26%)

ownership.holder: US/allied consortium (Oracle, Silver Lake, MGX ~15% each)
ownership.control: US-majority board; algorithm LICENSED TO ORACLE, retrained on US data; ByteDance 19.9%

influence_vectors.vector: State 'golden shares' / special-management-shares (~1% + board/content veto) in CHINA-DOMESTIC operating subs of ByteDance (Douyin/Toutiao), Alibaba, Tencent
influence_vectors.notes: Real and powerful, but reaches only China-domestic content/news licensing - NOT Western subsidiaries.

influence_vectors.vector: National Intelligence Law Art. 7 (compelled cooperation) + VIE structures
influence_vectors.notes: Creates theoretical leverage; no evidence used to direct Western child-safety lawfare.

influence_vectors.vector: Equity stakes used to steer Western online-safety policy
influence_vectors.notes: Most stakes are non-voting/passive; incentive to resist costly youth-safety rules is shared by ALL platform shareholders, not China-specific.

grade_reasoning: Cross-ownership is extensive and real but predominantly MINORITY, NON-VOTING, and PASSIVE (Riot 100% and Supercell ~82% are the exceptions). Hard CCP levers (golden shares, NIL, VIE) operate on China-DOMESTIC content/data, not Western subs. Critically, Beijing's own policy preference is STRICTER minor protection (anti-addiction/youth-mode), which cuts AGAINST the idea that China works to halt Western child-safety lawfare. Any incentive to resist costly youth-safety regulation is a generic platform-shareholder interest. Recurring incidental node: Oracle (Larry Ellison) now operates the TikTok US algorithm - another appearance of Oracle across AI-core / TikTok / (via Ellison) digital-ID policy.
